summary: Comprehensive backend test of MSME Credit Risk Early Warning System. 29/29 pytest tests passed (100%). Covered auth (signup/login/me, invalid creds, unauthenticated), seed idempotency, borrowers list with search/sector/risk/sort/pagination, CRUD with role-based access (admin can create/delete, rm cannot), borrower details aggregation, CSV upload with auto-recompute, risk recompute, notes (create/list + RBAC), AI chat with Claude Sonnet via emergentintegrations (request+history persistence), PDF/DOCX report generation (verified magic bytes), portfolio overview + analytics, alerts list and mark-read.

minor.issue: Credentials in /app/memory/test_credentials.md list rm/rm123 (5 chars) but UserSignup model enforces password min_length=6, causing 422. Either bump the doc/seed to a >=6 char password (e.g. rm12345) or relax the model. Tests use rm12345 to work around.

action_items: Update /app/memory/test_credentials.md (and any default-RM seed) to use a >=6 char password (e.g. rm12345) so the documented credentials are actually usable via /api/auth/signup.
Optional: consider returning 409 (not 400) for duplicate signup email for REST correctness.
Optional: portfolio_overview loads all borrowers into memory for aggregation; fine for current scale but switch to MongoDB aggregation pipeline if borrower count grows large.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~580 lines; still manageable but would benefit from being split into routers per domain (auth, borrowers, portfolio, chat, reports) as the codebase grows.

_recompute_and_persist deletes & re-inserts all warning_signals on every recompute and appends a new alerts batch each call - this can rapidly grow the alerts collection on repeated uploads/recomputes. Consider de-duplication or upsert based on (borrower_id, alert_type, day).

Borrower list endpoint trusts sort_by directly from query string and passes to Mongo .sort(); not a security issue but invalid field names will silently sort by nothing - validate against an allowlist.
JWT_SECRET has a non-fatal default 'dev-secret' in auth.py while .env provides a value; fine for dev but ensure prod fails fast if missing.
POST /api/seed is gated only by get_current_user - any authenticated user (including rm) can trigger seeding. Consider restricting to admin via require_role('admin').
All endpoints correctly exclude Mongo _id via projections; no ObjectId serialization issues found.
Role-based access correctly enforced on POST/PUT/DELETE /borrowers, POST /borrowers/{id}/upload, POST /notes (admin/analyst only) and DELETE /borrowers (admin only).
test_credentials: admin@msme.com/admin123 (pre-seeded), analyst@msme.com/analyst123 (auto-created by test), rm@msme.com/rm12345 (auto-created by test; doc lists rm123 but min_length=6 forces use of rm12345)
seed_data_creation: Used existing /api/seed (idempotent, 12 seeded borrowers). Test data is prefixed TEST_ and the created borrower is deleted within the run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 29 backend tests pass cleanly. Test suite lives at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. Use admin@msme.com/admin123 for admin. RM password must be >=6 chars (use rm12345). The chat test calls Claude Sonnet via Emergent LLM key and adds ~5s latency; rest is fast (<10s total). If repeated runs cause alerts table bloat, that's expected per code review note above.
rca of the issue: N/A - no failing functional issues. Only credential-doc mismatch (rm123 vs min_length=6) and minor design notes captured under action_items/critical_code_review_comments.
